basics.name: Hyeonseo Oh
basics.label: Autonomous Driving & Control Systems Researcher
basics.summary: Autonomous driving and control researcher specializing in safety-critical systems. Experienced in Robust Control Barrier Functions (CBF), MPC/MPPI, and real-time simulation. Co-author of an IEEE submission on uncertainty-aware CBF. Hands-on with ROS2 racing platforms, LiDAR/vision perception, and embedded motor control.

work.name: Machine Learning and Control Systems (MLCS) Lab, Yonsei University
work.summary: Research on safety-critical control, robust CBF, MPPI, and autonomous racing.
highlights: Designed a Robust CBF framework addressing worst-case state-estimation error and stochastic disturbances.
Co-author of IEEE L-CSS + CDC submission: 'Efficient and Robust Safety Control via CBFs Exploiting Worst-case Error Dynamics'.
Developing E2E racing controller integrated with Game-theoretic CBF for multi-agent racing.
Mitigated MPPI over-conservativeness in curves and over-aggressiveness on straights (Research Poster).
Completed 4-week intensive program: Python, MLP/CNN/RNN, LQR, MPC, QP, RL, and mobile robot experiments.

publications.name: Trajectory-Level Safety Verification for Multi-Modal End-to-End Planners via Post-Planning Re-ranking
publications.summary: 1st Author. Verifies and re-ranks the 6 trajectory candidates output by E2E models (VAD) for real safety guarantees. Uses Autoware as the experimental platform.

publications.name: Efficient and Robust Safety Control via CBFs Exploiting Worst-case Error Dynamics
publications.summary: Co-author. Designed Robust CBF for ACC, Half-space safety, and Collision Avoidance. Derived closed-form QCQP solution using KKT conditions.

projects.name: A1 Autonomous Driving Challenge
projects.description: Overall Team Lead. Developing Neuro-Strategic E2E racing model combining strategy and safety. Completed solo driving logic via Imitation Learning; currently applying Reinforcement Learning.

projects.name: KSAE Formula Autonomous Competition
projects.description: Team Lead for full autonomous driving logic. Perception: RANSAC + DBSCAN cone classification, LiDAR-Camera fusion via HSV + Kalman Filter. Planning & Control: Cubic Spline, Curvature-based speed planning, Pure Pursuit + PID.

projects.name: 4th F1Tenth Korea Championship
projects.description: PCB board design & SW logic. Applied Reinforcement Learning (~1M steps in simulation) with SLAM-based map generation. Explored sim-to-real transfer challenges.

projects.name: Kookmin University Autonomous Driving Competition
projects.description: Team Lead. Lane keeping using BEV + HSV filtering + Sliding Window. Applied adaptive PID control based on road curvature.

projects.name: HL FMA Autonomous Driving Competition
projects.description: SW Team Lead. YOLOv8-based traffic light detection, RTK GPS with UTM coordinate conversion, GPS heading + coordinate derivative fusion for stability. Encouragement Award.

projects.name: Electromagnetic Analysis of Electric Motor (FEMM)
projects.description: FEM-based simulation of DC/BLDC motors. Analyzed flux density, torque ripple, and efficiency across design parameters. Exported results to MATLAB for torque-speed curve analysis.

projects.name: Lead-Lag Controller Design (LabVIEW)
projects.description: Measured frequency response with DSA equipment. Designed Lead-Lag controller in MATLAB targeting ~30 Hz crossover frequency with sufficient phase margin. Validated via step response on LabVIEW real-time control system.

awards.title: Encouragement Award
awards.summary: SW Team Lead. 1/5 scale autonomous vehicle — traffic light, parking, lane keeping, obstacle missions.

awards.title: Encouragement Award (Individual)
awards.summary: Designed cultural heritage goods — earphone holder in the shape of a gayageum.

awards.title: Outstanding Support Staff Award
awards.summary: Recognized for excellence in assisting visually impaired students.

volunteer.summary: Co-founded club. Participating in autonomous driving competitions, latest paper studies, simulation-based experiments, and building AI/autonomous driving research culture at Yonsei.
Weekly math mentoring for children at a community welfare center in Seodaemun-gu.
Assisted visually impaired students with coursework and produced braille materials. Received Outstanding Support Staff Award.

certificates.name: LG Aimers 6th Cohort
certificates.summary: Completed AI fundamentals course and AI hackathon on IVF pregnancy success prediction.
